scan.zone_map_skip: skip block b if zm[b].min_uom > usd_code || zm[b].max_uom < usd_code
scan.prefetch: software prefetch 16 rows ahead: __builtin_prefetch(gid_ptr + adsh_ptr[r+16], 0, 1) to overlap L3 latency for gid_ptr[adsh_code] random lookups

filters.notes: sub_fy flat array (172KB) resident in L1; O(1) lookup via gid_ptr sentinel: gid_ptr[ac] < 0 means fy!=2022, no separate sub_fy lookup needed in hot loop

join_order.notes: adsh_code == sub row index; gid_ptr[adsh_code] < 0 sentinel encodes fy!=2022

data_structures.sub_fy_array: flat_array int16_t[86135] — loaded once, used in build_compact_indexes only
data_structures.adsh_group_id: flat_array int32_t[86135] — compact group index (0..n_groups-1) or -1 if fy!=2022; built during build_compact_indexes using uint64_t hash key
data_structures.all_grp: flat_array int64_t[nthreads × n_groups] — thread-major layout; thread t's block at offset t*n_groups; per-thread group accumulator in cents (int64_t); NO all_cik array
data_structures.group_sum: flat_array int64_t[n_groups] — merged from all_grp via sequential t-outer-g-inner loop
data_structures.cik_sum: flat_array int64_t[n_ciks] — computed POST-MERGE from group_sum in O(n_groups) pass; NOT accumulated during scan

parallelism.scan_schedule: schedule(static) — uniform block size, static partitioning eliminates atomic scheduling overhead
parallelism.merge_strategy: sequential t-outer g-inner: for t in 0..nthreads: lp=all_grp+t*n_groups; for g in 0..n_groups: group_sum[g]+=lp[g]. Each iteration is a 200KB sequential scan. Total 12.8MB sequential reads vs 21ms of strided random L3 access in prior iteration.
parallelism.notes: Eliminate all_cik (5.1MB) entirely. Drop lc[] update from scan hot loop. After group_sum merge: for each gid in 0..n_groups, cik_sum[groups[gid].cik_idx] += group_sum[gid]. This is O(n_groups)=O(17K) vs O(34M) accumulation in scan.

fused_scan_logic.description: Single parallel pass computing ONLY group_sum (adsh-level) from identical filter. cik_sum computed post-merge from group_sum. Eliminates lc[] random write from hot loop.
fused_scan_logic.build_group_key: uint64_t key = (uint64_t)(uint32_t)cik << 32 | fnv1a32(name_ptr, name_len); use unordered_map<uint64_t,int32_t> — integer key is ~4x faster than std::string key. GroupInfo stores {rep_adsh, cik, cik_idx}.
fused_scan_logic.inner_loop: const int PFETCH=16; for r in block: if r+PFETCH<row_end: __builtin_prefetch(gid_ptr+adsh_ptr[r+PFETCH],0,1); if uom_ptr[r]!=usd_code continue; ac=adsh_ptr[r]; gid=gid_ptr[ac]; if gid<0 continue; lg[gid]+=llround(val_ptr[r]*100.0)
fused_scan_logic.post_scan_merge: sequential: zero group_sum; for t=0..nthreads: lp=all_grp+t*n_groups; for g=0..n_groups: group_sum[g]+=lp[g]

having.threshold_source: sum(cik_sum[0..n_ciks-1]) / n_ciks (computed in O(n_groups) post-merge pass)

output.decode_step: for each gid where group_sum[gid] > avg_threshold_cents: name via name_offsets/name_data[groups[gid].rep_adsh], cik = groups[gid].cik

optimization_notes: Root cause: two problems identified post-iteration-1. (1) Merge bottleneck (~21ms unaccounted): the group_sum merge used OMP parallel-for with groups as outer loop and threads as inner loop — a column-major traversal of a row-major matrix (all_grp[t*n_groups+g]), generating 1.6M random L3 accesses with stride 200KB. Fix (Category B): swap to t-outer-g-inner sequential scan; each thread's 200KB block is read sequentially, converting random L3 access to 12.8MB streaming reads (~0.3ms at 50 GB/s). (2) Redundant cik accumulation in scan (Category C): the hot loop did 2 random writes per qualifying row (lg[gid] and lc[cik_idx]). Since cik_sum = sum of group_sum values per cik, it can be computed from group_sum in O(n_groups)=O(17K) post-merge instead of O(34M) in-scan, eliminating the lc[] write and the 5.1MB all_cik allocation entirely. (3) Faster group_map (Category A): replace std::unordered_map<string,int32_t> with unordered_map<uint64_t,int32_t> using key=(cik<<32)|fnv1a32(name) — integer key hash/compare eliminates string allocation and is ~4x faster. (4) Explicit prefetch: software prefetch gid_ptr 16 rows ahead to overlap L3 latency for data-dependent random lookups. Expected: merge ~21ms->~2ms, build_joins ~6ms->~2ms, scan ~26ms->~21ms, total ~25ms.
